title: OpenBodhi Node
description: A single knowledge node in the OpenBodhi vault

id.description: UUID v4. Generated on creation. Immutable.

type.description: Node type. Determines how the node is treated by workers.

content.description: The raw thought. Preserved as captured. Never edited by AI.

energy_level.description: Inferred urgency/resonance from language. 1 = low, 5 = high. Never prompted.

content_enriched.description: Legible expansion of content by local Mistral Nemo model. Preserves original meaning. Used for clustering and similarity, not displayed.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the original content field. Computed on write. Immutable.

enriched_at.description: Timestamp of enrichment completion by the Enricher worker.

enrichment_model.description: Name of the local model used for enrichment, e.g. mistral-nemo:12b.

related_papers.description: Research concepts matched by the Enricher from the hard-coded reference library.

updated_at.description: Last modification timestamp. Set by workers on update.

promoted_from.description: If this node was promoted from another (e.g. Idea -> Pattern), the source node UUID.

cluster_id.description: HDBSCAN cluster label assigned by Surveyor. Format: cluster-<int> or noise.

embedding_model.description: Name of the embedding model used to vectorize this node.

created_by.description: Which worker or human created this node.

media_type.description: Media type that produced this node. Defaults to text for plain messages. Set by Curator on intake.

media_ref.description: Reference to original media file. Telegram file_id for images/voice/video, URL for links/documents. Used by workers needing the source media.

domain.description: Primary wellness domain. Set by Curator based on content analysis. Used by Distiller for grouping and Surveyor for cross-domain bridge detection.

people.description: People mentioned in this node (names, handles, roles). Extracted by Curator. Used by Surveyor to detect social bridges and relational patterns across nodes.

social_context.description: Social context of the observation. solo = alone, social = friends/family, professional = work/healthcare, intimate = close relationship. Inferred by Curator from content when people are present.
